description:
You are the release-manager for a small company.  
    Two draft release descriptors already exist in  

      /home/user/releases/input/release-a.json  
      /home/user/releases/input/release-b.json  

    Your job is to 1) publish a formal JSON-Schema for those files, 2) run a lightweight jq-based
    validation pass, and 3) generate a deployability summary that other tooling will later
    consume.

    Perform the following steps entirely from the terminal (≈ 5–10 commands is plenty; use any
    mix of shell, jq, echo, cat, etc.):

    1. Create the directory /home/user/releases/output/ (if it does not already exist).
    2. Inside /home/user/releases/ write a file called release.schema.json that meets **all** of
       the following exact requirements (the automated test will compare it byte-for-byte):
       • It must be valid Draft-07 JSON-Schema.  
       • Top-level required keys: version, services.  
       • version: string, must match the pattern ^v[0-9]+$  
       • services: non-empty array.  Each element is an object that must contain  
         name (string), image (string) and replicas (integer ≥ 1).  
       • No additional keys are forbidden (i.e. additionalProperties not specified).  
       (Indent with two spaces; terminate the last line with a UNIX newline.)

    3. Using jq only (do **not** install extra software), run a simple presence-check for the
       two required top-level keys inside every *.json file in /home/user/releases/input/.
       Produce a plain-text log file at  

          /home/user/releases/output/validation.log  

       For each file processed, add **one** line to that log in the exact format  

          FILENAME: VALID  

       or  

          FILENAME: INVALID  

       where FILENAME is the base-name (e.g. release-a.json).  The automated test will look
       for exactly two lines, in **alphabetical order by file name**, each followed by a single
       UNIX newline character.  Both current drafts should validate successfully.

    4. Still using jq, extract a unique, alphabetically sorted list of every service.name that
       appears in any input file.  Write that list as prettified JSON to  

          /home/user/releases/output/service-summary.json  

       The file must look **exactly** like:

       {
         "services": [
           "auth",
           "billing",
           "search"
         ]
       }

       (Two-space indentation, trailing newline.)

    5. Make no other filesystem changes.

    NOTE:
    • All paths are under /home/user; you do not have root access.  
    • The autograder will only scan the three files explicitly mentioned above and will compare
      them byte-for-byte to its expectations, so follow the required formatting precisely.  
    • Feel free to print any console output you need while you work—only the contents of the
      three files will be scored.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing filesystem setup -->
  Directory /home/user/releases/input/ already exists.

  File /home/user/releases/input/release-a.json:
  {
    "version": "v1",
    "services": [
      { "name": "auth",    "image": "registry.example.com/auth:1.2.3",    "replicas": 2 },
      { "name": "billing", "image": "registry.example.com/billing:3.4.5", "replicas": 1 }
    ]
  }

  File /home/user/releases/input/release-b.json:
  {
    "version": "v2",
    "services": [
      { "name": "auth",   "image": "registry.example.com/auth:1.2.4",   "replicas": 3 },
      { "name": "search", "image": "registry.example.com/search:2.1.0", "replicas": 2 }
    ]
  }

  --------------------------------------------------------------------
  Expected artefacts **after** the agent finishes:

  1. /home/user/releases/release.schema.json
     Exact content (including final newline):

{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "required": [
    "version",
    "services"
  ],
  "properties": {
    "version": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^v[0-9]+$"
    },
    "services": {
      "type": "array",
      "minItems": 1,
      "items": {
        "type": "object",
        "required": [
          "name",
          "image",
          "replicas"
        ],
        "properties": {
          "name": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "image": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "replicas": {
            "type": "integer",
            "minimum": 1
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

  2. /home/user/releases/output/validation.log
     Exact content (including trailing newline on each line):
release-a.json: VALID
release-b.json: VALID

  3. /home/user/releases/output/service-summary.json
     Exact content (including final newline):

{
  "services": [
    "auth",
    "billing",
    "search"
  ]
}

  --------------------------------------------------------------------
  The automated tests will:
    • Confirm that the three files above exist with the exact byte-for-byte content specified.
    • Ignore any additional files or console output produced by the agent.
